shape_decode: cmp=L, vp=W, vpc=W, naive=W -> cmplog BLOCKS; value_profile + value_profile_cmplog + naive RESOLVE. Mixed shape: each branch carries an i2s-ANTI claim (_h0: naive>cmplog, I2S hurts) AND a vp-PRO claim (_h1: vpc>cmplog, CMP_MAP gradient rescues). Each hypothesis is assigned by its named mechanism+direction, not by the shape label.
round_note: Loop-until-dry ROUND 2 re-design. SUPERSET of the round-1 evidence_test.json (preserved at evidence_test.r0.json is the original; this file's prior version was round 1). The 8 CONFIRMED branches' hypotheses are KEPT verbatim so they re-validate unchanged. The 3 STILL-UNLABELED branches (libpng/3974, harfbuzz/5977, harfbuzz/5998) had their round-1 tests (depth_reach for 5977/5998; value_distance_reached for libpng/3974) SCORED in arbitration and NONE held (rule_not_met). Round 2 may NOT re-propose those rules with relaxed thresholds. For harfbuzz/5977+5998 a GENUINELY NEW mechanism (structural-token / seed-size content, via joint_necessity + a new corpus_size_ratio descriptor) is proposed -- a different signal than the refuted iteration-depth one. For libpng/3974 NO built or buildable descriptor can discriminate its mechanism (raw-havoc-throughput on a DERIVED overflow sentinel with no input preimage), so it is honestly marked decidable:false (G3).

hypotheses.label: I2S RandReplace overwrites the exact structural/category/literal bytes naive havoc assembles, depleting (not enriching) the target byte in cmplog's corpus relative to naive

params.note: harfbuzz on-disk corpus available; these branches expose a fixed-ish offset structural/category byte (Halant H bytes, VPre record bytes, the repeated-record stride) that I2S substitution displaces.

hypotheses.prediction: If I2S is ACTIVELY CORRUPTING the structural byte, cmplog's corpus is byte-DEPLETED at the target offset vs naive => signed_target_enrich < 0 (anti). An I2S-IRRELEVANT gate (the no-preimage class) returns no_gate_signature; a pro-I2S gate returns > 0. A clearly NEGATIVE enrichment discriminates active corruption from mere irrelevance.
hypotheses.g1_alternative_ruled_out: Rules out 'I2S has no traction' (no_gate_signature) and 'I2S helps' (positive enrich): only ACTIVE depletion of the structural byte in the cmplog corpus produces signed_target_enrich < -0.3.
hypotheses.g2_independence: operand_enrichment compares cmplog-vs-naive corpus byte frequency, never the vp/vpc resolve signal that defined the shape.

hypotheses.label: Gate has no input-side CMP preimage (category-table / state-machine classification, length threshold, or internally-derived overflow sentinel); I2S substitution finds no fixed-offset value to copy, so it misallocates budget while naive havoc explores freely
hypotheses.membership_change_note: libpng_3974_h0 REMOVED vs round 1: although it is a genuine no-preimage gate (the 0x7fffffff sentinel has no input byte to copy), its i2s-anti mechanism per the card is RAW HAVOC THROUGHPUT (naive runs more execs/sec with no CMP tax), which operand_enrichment's no_gate_signature outcome does NOT positively confirm -- a throughput advantage and an operand-absent gate are not the same claim, and no built tool measures throughput. libpng_3974 is now placed in decidable_false (see below) rather than asserted as a no-preimage confirmation it cannot earn. The 5 remaining members are harfbuzz operand-absent gates whose no_gate_signature outcome IS the intended positive signal and which were already confirmed.

params.note: operand_enrichment returns no_gate_signature on structural/length gates and on gates with no fixed-offset value operand.

hypotheses.prediction: These gates have NO fixed-offset input-byte value to substitute (category-table dispatch, syllable-length threshold start+3<=end). operand_enrichment returns no_gate_signature. A genuine fixed-offset value gate would instead return a definite signed_target_enrich; so 'no_gate_signature' positively discriminates the no-preimage class from byte-corruption.
hypotheses.g1_alternative_ruled_out: Distinguishes the no-input-preimage class (no fixed-offset operand => no_gate_signature) from the active-corruption class (a real target byte => a numeric signed enrich). If the tool DID find a target offset and return a number, this hypothesis is REFUTED for that branch.
hypotheses.g2_independence: Corpus-byte-frequency measurement, independent of the vp/vpc resolve pattern.

hypotheses.label: VP CMP_MAP Hamming/prefix-distance gradient gives graded partial-progress feedback toward the gate comparand, letting value_profile/vpc retain near-miss inputs that approach the target while cmplog's edge-only binary feedback stalls

params.note: Comparands: category/codepoint equality states (harfbuzz indic/myanmar/USE category gates, 0x0D12 MALAYALAM codepoint).

hypotheses.prediction: If VP's gradient is the rescue mechanism, value_profile's corpus REACHES strictly closer to the gate comparand than cmplog's (vp_min_distance < cmp_min_distance, winner_closer=true) while cmplog plateaus far from the target. If both reached equal distance, the rescue is NOT gradient-driven (refuted).
hypotheses.g1_alternative_ruled_out: Rules out 'value_profile resolves for a non-gradient reason' (e.g. raw throughput): under that alternative vp and cmplog reach equal value-distance and distance_gap ~ 0. A gradient rescue REQUIRES vp to approach measurably closer.
hypotheses.g2_independence: Measures corpus value-distance to the comparand, NOT the vpc-resolves flag that defined the shape.

hypotheses.label: For the harfbuzz USE-shaper structural-assembly gates (5977 ZWNJ-plus-following-non-ignorable loop-exit; 5998 halant-bearing reorder syllable), the gate is reached only by a buffer carrying intact, readable script/language TAG substrings ('srch','jy-hant','nt-hk') that map to real USE glyph categories. cmplog's I2SRandReplace continually overwrites those readable tag bytes with CMP-buffer operands, so its blocking corpus is dominated by repeated CMP-derived byte RUNS that lack the structural-token content, whereas naive's plain havoc keeps and recombines the intact tag substrings -- an I2S-anti effect measurable as a deficit in structural-token COUNT and tag-bearing seed SIZE in cmplog's corpus relative to naive's, NOT as a fixed-offset byte depletion and NOT as a per-seed branch iteration-depth (the refuted round-1 signal).
hypotheses.novelty_basis: GENUINELY different mechanism and signal from every prior i2s-anti hypothesis tried on these branches. byte_corruption asserts a fixed-offset byte-frequency depletion (operand_enrichment) -- these gates have no fixed offset, so it returns no_gate_signature, non-discriminating. no_preimage asserts that same no_gate_signature as a positive signal -- non-discriminating here (it cannot tell structural-state gates apart from each other). The ROUND-1 structural_state_undermutation hypothesis asserted a per-seed branch iteration-DEPTH deficit (depth_reach: winner_deeper, depth_lift>=1.3) and was SCORED + REFUTED (rule_not_met). THIS hypothesis makes a DIFFERENT, falsifiable claim over a DIFFERENT built signal: the structural-TOKEN CONTENT and seed SIZE of the cmplog-blocking vs naive-resolving corpus (joint_necessity's struct_size_and_token_count metrics), i.e. it is about what readable tag-derived structure the seeds CONTAIN, not how deep they iterate the branch. It is therefore not a relaxed-threshold re-test of any prior rule.

params.note: joint_necessity reports median seed size + structural-token count for the resolving vs blocking seed sets. Here the CONTRAST is naive-resolving vs cmplog-BLOCKING (the i2s-anti axis): cmp_tags = structural-token count carried by cmplog's blocking seeds; we predict it is depressed vs the resolving set, and size_lift (resolving/blocking median seed size) > 1 because tag-bearing assembled buffers are larger than cmplog's CMP-noise runs. harfbuzz on-disk corpus + cov available on s4.
params.tokens: USE/OpenType script-and-language TAG substrings the cards name as the structural carriers: srch, jy-hant, an-hant, nt-hk (4-byte ASCII OpenType-style tag runs); generally readable [a-z-]{3,7} tag substrings that decode to real USE glyph categories, as opposed to the high-entropy repeated CMP-derived byte runs (e.g. 00 72 10 repeats) that dominate cmplog's blocking seeds.

hypotheses.prediction: If I2S is starving the structural-token content, cmplog's BLOCKING seeds carry materially FEWER readable tag tokens than naive's RESOLVING seeds (cmp_tags depressed) AND the resolving tag-bearing buffers are larger (size_lift >= 1.3). If cmplog's blocking corpus carried the SAME structural-token count (cmp_tags ~ resolving set) the I2S-starvation story is REFUTED -- cmplog assembled the tag structure and stalled for another reason. A clear token-content deficit positively confirms the structural-token-starvation mechanism.
hypotheses.g1_alternative_ruled_out: Rules out 'cmplog assembles the same tag structure but loses for a value/throughput reason' (under which cmp_tags ~ the resolving set and size_lift ~ 1, so the rule fails). It also distinguishes this class from the refuted iteration-DEPTH story (depth_reach asked how deep the branch iterates; this asks what tag-derived structure the seeds CONTAIN) and from byte_corruption (fixed-offset byte deficit). Only an actual structural-token-content deficit in cmplog's corpus confirms it.
hypotheses.g2_independence: joint_necessity measures the structural-token count + median size of the SEED SETS (a content property of the corpus), not the cmp=L/naive=W resolve flags that defined the shape. The shape says naive resolves and cmplog blocks; this asks WHY -- whether cmplog's seeds simply never carry the tag-derived glyph-category structure -- an independent question.

hypotheses.label: For the same harfbuzz USE structural-assembly gates (5977/5998), value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient rewards partial progress toward the byte patterns selecting the ZWNJ-plus-non-ignorable / halant glyph categories, so the vpc-resolving corpus accretes MORE structural-token (tag-derived) content than cmplog's blocking corpus -- the gradient-rescue mechanism expressed as a structural-token-CONTENT lift (joint_necessity) rather than the refuted per-seed iteration-DEPTH lift (depth_reach), because there is no fixed scalar operand for value_distance_reached to climb.
hypotheses.novelty_basis: The kept vp_pro_cmpmap_gradient_rescue measures Hamming-closure to a concrete category/codepoint comparand via value_distance_reached; that tool has NOTHING to measure for 5977/5998 (no operand_literal). The ROUND-1 vp_pro_structural_state_depth_climb measured the gradient as a per-seed branch iteration-DEPTH lift (depth_reach) and was SCORED + REFUTED (rule_not_met). THIS hypothesis carries the same VP-gradient story but measures it as a structural-token CONTENT lift in the vpc-resolving vs cmplog-blocking corpus (joint_necessity vpc_tags vs cmp_tags + size_lift) -- a different built signal (what structure the corpus contains, not how deep it iterates), so it is not a relaxed-threshold re-run of the refuted depth rule.

params.note: joint_necessity's vpc_tags (structural-token count of the vpc-RESOLVING seeds) vs cmp_tags (cmplog-BLOCKING seeds); tag_lift = vpc/cmp token ratio. We interrogate the vpc-vs-cmplog contrast (VP-pro). harfbuzz on-disk corpus available on s4.
params.tokens: Same USE/OpenType tag substrings as the i2s-anti hypothesis: srch, jy-hant, an-hant, nt-hk and readable [a-z-]{3,7} tag runs decoding to real USE glyph categories.

hypotheses.prediction: If VP's gradient accretes the structural-token content, vpc's resolving seeds carry MORE tag tokens than cmplog's blocking seeds: vpc_tags > cmp_tags, tag_lift >= 1.3 (and size_lift >= 1.3). If both carry equal token content (tag_lift ~ 1), the vpc win is not gradient-driven structural accretion and this VP-pro hypothesis is REFUTED for the branch.
hypotheses.g1_alternative_ruled_out: Rules out 'vpc carries the same structural-token content as cmplog but resolves for a non-gradient reason' (tag_lift ~ 1, rule fails). A confirmed VP-pro structural climb REQUIRES vpc to carry measurably more tag-derived structure than cmplog -- the gradient is doing content-accretion work cmplog's edge-only feedback cannot. Caveat: vpc-resolving seeds inherently sit at-or-past the gate, so the discriminating power comes from cmplog's blocking seeds carrying FEWER tokens, not merely from vpc carrying many.
hypotheses.g2_independence: joint_necessity measures structural-token content + size of the seed sets, not the vpc-RESOLVES flag that defined the shape. The shape says vpc resolves; this asks WHETHER vpc's seeds carry more accumulated tag-derived structure -- an independent question.

decision_order_rationale: Per branch, the _h0 (i2s-anti) and _h1 (vp-pro) axes are INDEPENDENT and BOTH may confirm. i2s-anti axis order: (1) byte_corruption (definite negative enrich at a fixed offset); (2) structural_token_content_starvation (token-content + size deficit, for the structural-assembly gates with no fixed offset, NEW for 5977/5998); (3) no_preimage (no_gate_signature, operand-absent residue). vp-pro axis: (4) cmpmap_gradient_rescue (category/codepoint comparands via value_distance_reached); (5) structural_token_content_climb (token-content lift for 5977/5998, NEW). A branch can be evidence-confirmed on one axis, both, or neither; unconfirmed-on-both falls to fallback=inconclusive. libpng_3974 is on neither list -- see decidable_false.
data_realities_respected: I2S is untagged in mutation_op: NO seed_lineage 'I2S event' descriptor proposed. operand_enrichment uses corpus BYTE FREQUENCY; the NEW i2s_anti_structural_token_content_starvation uses joint_necessity's seed-set structural-TOKEN COUNT + median seed SIZE -- neither is a lineage event.

value_profile is a FEEDBACK not a mutator: both vp-pro hypotheses measure what the corpus REACHES/CONTAINS (value_distance_reached Hamming-closure; joint_necessity token content), never a lineage gradient event.

Structural-assembly gates have different-SIZED W/L seeds so byte-alignment (byte_freq_ratio) fails: the two structural-token hypotheses for 5977/5998 explicitly use joint_necessity (struct_size_and_token_count), which is the data_realities-sanctioned alternative (struct_size_ratio / token_count) for different-sized seeds.

On-disk corpora + cov images: harfbuzz is on s4 (corpus + libafl-harfbuzz-cov) -> all 10 harfbuzz branches scorable, INCLUDING the new joint_necessity tests. libpng is on sB (lcms/libxml2/libpng/bloaty); but libpng_3974 is decidable:false for a TOOL-COVERAGE reason (no descriptor fits its derived-sentinel/throughput mechanism), NOT merely because its corpus is absent on s4 -- the cross-server rule is honored (it would be decidable:false on the OWNING server too).

joint_necessity --tokens is target-specific: the harfbuzz tag substrings (srch/jy-hant/an-hant/nt-hk and readable [a-z-]{3,7} runs) are supplied as the structural-token vocabulary, exactly as the registry scope_limit (font tags for harfbuzz, SQL keywords for sqlite3) requires.

decidable_false.rationale: libpng/3974 is honestly NON-DISCRIMINABLE by any built or buildable descriptor, on BOTH axes, for tool-coverage (not corpus-absence) reasons -- so it stays inconclusive (G3). (a) _h0 i2s-anti: the card's mechanism is RAW HAVOC THROUGHPUT -- naive runs more executions/sec with no CMP-interception tax and stochastically stumbles on the sentinel. No built tool measures a throughput advantage; operand_enrichment returns a non-discriminating no_gate_signature (true of every operand-absent gate, so it cannot positively confirm a throughput story); the gate is a single equality, not a loop/iteration gate, so depth_reach makes no sense; the seed sizes are about IDAT image-stream bulk, not the gate's structural assembly, so joint_necessity/corpus_size_ratio would measure an irrelevant size difference. (b) _h1 vp-pro: the gate comparand 0x7fffffff is a DERIVED OVERFLOW SENTINEL produced internally by png_get_fixed_point overflow logic -- it has NO input-byte pre-image. value_distance_reached measures Hamming distance of INPUT bytes to the literal, but no input-byte pattern corresponds to the derived sentinel, so its closure metric is meaningless here -- which is exactly why the round-1 value_distance_reached test was scored and did not hold. There is no built descriptor that climbs distance to a value the input never literally contains. Marking this decidable:false is the CORRECT outcome rather than forcing a third relaxed re-test of a tool that structurally cannot measure this gate.

notes.priors_kept: All round-1/round-0 KEEP hypotheses retained as a superset so the 8 confirmed branches re-validate: i2s_anti_structural_byte_corruption (verbatim rule), i2s_anti_no_input_preimage_misdirection (rule verbatim; libpng_3974_h0 removed from its membership because its no_gate_signature outcome was a NON-discriminating throughput case, now routed to decidable_false), vp_pro_cmpmap_gradient_rescue (verbatim). The round-1 NEW hypotheses that were SCORED+REFUTED for the 3 unlabeled branches (i2s_anti_structural_state_undermutation and vp_pro_structural_state_depth_climb over depth_reach; vp_pro_sentinel_value_climb over value_distance_reached) are NOT carried forward -- a refuted rule must not be re-proposed, even relaxed.
notes.new_mechanisms: TWO new hypotheses, both for harfbuzz_5977 + harfbuzz_5998, both over the BUILT joint_necessity tool (struct_size_and_token_count) which was NOT YET TRIED on this shape: (1) i2s_anti_structural_token_content_starvation -- I2S overwrites readable tag substrings so cmplog's blocking corpus is token-content-starved (cmp_tags < 1 AND size_lift >= 1.3); (2) vp_pro_structural_token_content_climb -- VP gradient accretes tag-derived structure so vpc's corpus is token-richer than cmplog's (tag_lift >= 1.3 AND size_lift >= 1.3). Both interrogate seed-set structural-TOKEN CONTENT, a genuinely different signal than the refuted iteration-DEPTH (depth_reach) round-1 tests.
notes.decidable_false: libpng_3974 (both _h0 and _h1) is honestly marked decidable:false for tool-coverage reasons: its i2s-anti mechanism is raw-havoc throughput (no built tool measures throughput) and its vp-pro comparand 0x7fffffff is a derived overflow sentinel with no input pre-image (value_distance_reached cannot climb distance to a value the input never literally holds). This is the same reason its round-1 value_distance_reached test failed. Per the cross-server rule this is NOT a corpus-absence mark -- it would be decidable:false on its owning server sB as well. It falls to fallback=inconclusive.
notes.why_round1_failed_for_the_3: harfbuzz_5977/5998: round-1 used depth_reach (per-seed branch iteration depth); arbitration scored 2/6 rules, none held -- the structural deficit is not visible as branch-line execution COUNT but (we now hypothesize) as structural-token CONTENT of the seeds. libpng_3974: round-1 used value_distance_reached on 0x7fffffff (4/6 rules scored, none held) -- the sentinel is a derived value with no input pre-image, so Hamming-closure over input bytes is structurally meaningless; no built tool can discriminate its real (throughput / derived-sentinel) mechanism, hence decidable:false this round.
notes.tools_used: operand_enrichment (2 kept i2s-anti hyps), value_distance_reached (1 kept vp-pro hyp), joint_necessity (2 NEW structural-token hyps for 5977/5998). joint_necessity was the registry's untried pivot for this shape. No unbuilt tool referenced.
notes.targets: 8 confirmed + harfbuzz_5977/5998 all on harfbuzz (s4: corpus + cov image). libpng_3974 (sB corpus) is decidable:false for tool-coverage, independent of server.
